subject_profile.summary: Highly educated American, board-certified laboratory animal veterinarian (ACLAM), considering relocation.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Scores predate full v2 source-discipline re-run and REQUIRE it most urgently.

CRITICAL: CCP-sourced data should be treated as UNUSABLE, not merely discounted. Economic figures and 'stability' scoring here partially absorbed regime self-presentation; flagged for rebuild from independent proxies (satellite nightlights, electricity, port traffic, foreign-firm earnings).

High-confidence China facts are disproportionately the negatives (institutions, civil liberties, demographics - documented by non-CCP sources); alleged strengths are disproportionately low-confidence CCP-sourced. True scores likely skew more negative.
NEGATIVE-SKEW case: demographic collapse largely locked in; authoritarian trajectory intensifying; no Ukraine-style positive-skew pathway.
Trade/industrial capacity is the most UNDER-weighted factor (5%) relative to its real importance; reweighting it up would improve economic score ~1.4 points.

Under stricter foundational-factor weighting (per subject's stated values), China likely drops BELOW the US, as its rule-of-law and civil-liberties scores are worse despite better infrastructure/safety.

economic.notes: Low confidence due to data unreliability. Massive industrial capacity (under-weighted) offset by debt, deflation, property collapse, no central-bank independence. Long-term lean more negative.

fiscal_state.notes: Non-financial debt ~296% GDP (Q3 2025); deficit >=4% GDP; local-govt debt from collapsed land sales; debt mostly domestic/RMB so self-restructurable.

monetary_independence.notes: PBoC is an arm of the Party-state; coordination but no institutional check; managing deflation (worse than mild inflation in debt-heavy economy).

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: RMB is the rising dollar-alternative (CIPS, swap lines, trade settlement) but capital controls structurally cap true reserve status.

inflation.notes: Deflation, not inflation; persistent, indicating weak domestic demand; raises real debt burden.

banking_stability.notes: State-dominated; stability through control masking property bad debt and shadow-banking risk.

trade_industrial_capacity.notes: Greatest strength and most under-weighted: manufacturing dominance, high-tech export surge, EV/battery/critical-mineral control, 43% GDP capital formation. Exposed to US decoupling; diversified to Global South.

institutional.notes: Worst institutional score of any country assessed. Scores at the bottom on exactly the factors the subject prioritizes (rule of law, freedom from arbitrary state action).

rule_of_law.notes: No independent judiciary; rule BY law not rule OF law; property/contracts contingent on Party tolerance; foreigners face arbitrary action, detention, exit bans.

statistical_integrity.notes: Official statistics unusable (5% official vs 2-3% independent-estimate growth); data managed for politics; youth-unemployment stat suspended when inconvenient.

civil_service_capacity.notes: High technocratic execution capacity, but increasingly politicized (loyalty over competence, anti-corruption as control, military purges).

press_freedom.notes: Among most restrictive globally; comprehensive censorship; no independent media; journalist surveillance/detention.

political_social.notes: Near-term authoritarian stability (suppressed instability) over catastrophic demographics and bottom-tier civil liberties. 'Stability' scoring flagged as partially absorbing regime framing. Long-term ~-5.

political_stability.notes: Stable near-term through control; long-term succession risk (no Xi successor, term limits removed) and authoritarian brittleness.

civil_liberties.notes: Comprehensive surveillance state, social credit, restrictions on speech/assembly/religion (Uyghur persecution), movement; no recourse against state.

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Structurally closed to immigration; PR extremely rare; no practical citizenship path; ethnonationalist turn; exit-ban risk; rising nationalism.

social_cohesion.notes: Low violent crime, strong order (via surveillance); underlying tensions (ethnic, urban-rural, youth disillusion, 'lying flat', high youth unemployment).

demographics.notes: Among worst of any major economy; births -17% 2024->2025; fertility <1.0; ~60M population decline 2026-2035; 24 provinces shrinking; ethnonationalism blocks immigration remedy.

geopolitical.notes: Few genuine allies; surrounded by wary neighbors; capital controls are a present baseline reality for asset holders, not a tail risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: Few formal alliances (North Korea, Russia partner-of-convenience); transactional/dependency model (BRI); surrounded by wary or hostile neighbors.

conflict_involvement.notes: Taiwan dominates; 2026 invasion assessed unlikely (Xi prioritizes regime stability); sustained coercion; long-term Taiwan/SCS/India risk; military purges cut both ways.

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Comprehensive capital controls already in place; restricted capital exit; decoupling/secondary-sanction/Taiwan-scenario freeze risk; baseline reality not tail risk.

physical_practical.notes: Genuine strengths in infrastructure and (surveillance-derived) street safety; healthcare mixed and aging-strained; the safety positive carries a civil-liberties cost counted elsewhere.

climate.notes: Northern water scarcity, air pollution (improving), extreme weather, agricultural stress; major renewable/adaptation investment.

healthcare.notes: Excellent urban facilities, weaker rural; universal coverage with gaps and OOP costs; severe aging strain ahead.

infrastructure.notes: Arguably world's best for new infrastructure: HSR, airports, ports, telecom, smart-city, renewables. Externally observable strength.

crime_safety.notes: Very low violent crime, strong street safety (surveillance-state artifact); real QoL positive setting aside civil-liberties cost.

disaster_insurance.notes: Quake/flood exposure; high state response capacity; developing insurance markets.

personal_fit.notes: Large, growing biomedical/CRO sector (WuXi AppTec et al.) with real demand for lab animal expertise, BUT: limited ACLAM recognition, different regulatory frame, severe language barrier, no-path-to-belonging for foreigners, ethical/regulatory environment differences. Scored +1 near / 0 long.

labanimal_vet_fit.notes: Work exists at scale but fit complicated by language, regulatory difference, foreigner-status issues, exit-ban risk.

summary: Scores poorly on exactly the factors the subject prioritizes: worst institutional score (-6.1) of any country, bottom-tier civil liberties, structurally closed to immigrants, catastrophic demographics. Real strengths (infrastructure +6, safety +5, manufacturing) are in dimensions the subject deprioritized and partly purchased via the civil-liberties failures. Barely better than the US for living (-3.77 vs -4.39), reaching that position via authoritarian baseline rather than democratic erosion. NEGATIVE skew (locked-in demographics, intensifying authoritarianism) is the inverse of Ukraine's positive skew. CCP data caveat means true scores likely worse; under strict foundational weighting China likely falls below the US.
